title: teucrium aureum subsp. latifolium
intro: Teucrium aureum subsp. latifolium, also known as the Golden Germander, is a subspecies of the Teucrium aureum plant, a type of flowering herb native to the Mediterranean region. The Golden Germander has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. Its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and alleviating symptoms of respiratory and skin conditions. The herbal preparations of Teucrium aureum subsp. latifolium include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
